title: No Matter What Happens to the Market, These 3 Dividend Stocks Belong in Your Portfolio
author: Justin Pope
description: The article recommends three Dividend King stocks (companies with 50+ consecutive years of dividend increases) as recession-resistant portfolio holdings: Altria Group for its pricing power despite declining smoking rates, Walmart for its dominant retail position and e-commerce growth, and Coca-Cola for its global beverage dominance and consistent earnings growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 56 consecutive years of dividend increases despite declining volumes, strong pricing power from addictive product, manageable payout ratio, and robust 5.9% dividend yield make it a reliable long-term holding.

53 consecutive dividend increases, dominant market position with 90% of Americans within short drive, successful e-commerce adaptation, and projected 9-10% annual earnings growth provide strong fundamentals for dividend growth.

64 consecutive years of dividend payments and increases, global distribution network, 2.2 billion daily servings, 7-8% projected annual earnings growth, and endorsement from Warren Buffett demonstrate resilience and reliability.
Mentioned as a holder of Coca-Cola stock through Warren Buffett's investment strategy, but not a primary recommendation in the article.
Mentioned as a holder of Coca-Cola stock through Warren Buffett's investment strategy, but not a primary recommendation in the article.
Mentioned only as a competitive reference point for Walmart's e-commerce efforts, not evaluated as an investment recommendation.

title: Market Crash: 3 Stocks I'd Buy Without Hesitation
author: Leo Sun
description: The article recommends three resilient blue-chip stocks to buy during market downturns: Walmart, a retail giant with 53 consecutive years of dividend increases; Realty Income, a REIT with 98.9% occupancy and monthly dividends; and Philip Morris International, a tobacco company transitioning to smoke-free products with strong growth prospects.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Praised as an evergreen retail stock with 53 consecutive years of dividend increases, strong e-commerce adaptation, and steady revenue/EPS growth projections of 5% and 9% CAGRs respectively through 2029.

Highlighted as one of the best REITs with exceptional 98.9% occupancy rate, 134 dividend increases since IPO, monthly dividend payments, and attractive 5.2% forward yield with expected 3-4% AFFO growth in 2026.

Recommended as a defensive dividend stock with strong fundamentals despite declining smoking rates; smoke-free revenue grew 14% organically and represents 43% of revenue, with projected 7% and 10% CAGRs for revenue and EPS through 2028.
Mentioned only as a competitor to Walmart in e-commerce and as a reference point for Walmart+ service; no direct investment recommendation provided.
Mentioned only as a competitor to Walmart's Sam's Club expansion; no direct investment recommendation provided.
Mentioned only as context for Philip Morris International's 2008 spinoff; no investment recommendation provided.

title: 3 Dividend Stocks to Hold for the Next 20 Years
author: Thomas Niel
description: The article recommends three dividend stocks positioned to become Dividend Kings: Mastercard benefits from global payment digitalization with 14 years of consecutive dividend growth averaging 10-15% annually; Microsoft has 24 years of dividend growth with over 10% annual increases and room to raise payouts further; Philip Morris International has diversified into smoke-free products with 18 years of consecutive dividend growth and potential for mid-single-digit future growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong tollbooth business model with high margins, 14 years of consecutive dividend growth (10-15% annually), and exposure to global shift from cash to digital payments, positioning it well for long-term dividend increases.
24 years of dividend growth with over 10% annual increases, low payout ratio (21%) providing room for future dividend expansion, and sustainable growth potential even if AI growth moderates.

18 years of consecutive dividend growth, successful diversification into smoke-free products (41.5% of revenues), double-digit earnings growth, and favorable consumer trends supporting mid-single-digit dividend growth potential.
Mentioned as a competitor to Mastercard with similar business model, but not the focus of the article's recommendation.
Mentioned only as the former parent company of Philip Morris International; not evaluated as an investment recommendation.

title: Better Stock to Buy Right Now: Altria vs. Coca-Cola
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: While Altria offers a higher dividend yield of 6.3% compared to Coca-Cola's 2.7%, Coca-Cola is the better choice for long-term dividend investors. Altria's core cigarette business is in decline with a 10% volume drop in 2025, and diversification efforts have resulted in billions in write-offs. Coca-Cola, meanwhile, demonstrates strong fundamentals with 1% case volume growth and 5% organic sales growth in 2025, backed by solid financials and a sustainable dividend.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Core cigarette business experiencing significant decline (10% volume drop in 2025), failed diversification attempts resulting in billions in write-offs, and high dividend yield reflects underlying business risk despite Dividend King status.

Strong business fundamentals with 1% case volume growth and 5% organic sales growth in 2025, globally dominant brand with loyal customers, solid financial position with investment-grade credit rating, and sustainable dividend with reasonable 66% payout ratio.

title: 4 Dividend Stocks to Double Up On Right Now
author: Leo Sun
description: The article recommends four dividend stocks as reliable income-generating investments: Chevron and Williams Companies, which benefit from rising energy prices, and Coca-Cola and Altria, which are resilient Dividend Kings despite facing headwinds in their core markets. All four stocks offer stable dividends and are positioned as safe-haven investments for long-term holders.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: 39 consecutive years of annual dividend increases, 3.8% forward yield, diversified operations across upstream/downstream/petrochemical markets, and positioned to benefit from rising oil prices with lower Middle East exposure.
Pure-play on natural gas market growth driven by cloud, data center, and AI expansion; 10 consecutive years of dividend increases; 2.86% dividend yield; stable toll-based business model.

Dividend King with 64 consecutive years of annual dividend increases; diversified beverage portfolio beyond declining soda sales; consistent price increases and share buybacks support EPS growth; 2.74% yield.

Dividend King with 60 dividend increases over 56 years; diversifying into smoke-free products targeting $5 billion revenue by 2028; highest yield among the four at 6.47%; strong pricing power and cost management.
